figures.caption: FIG. 8. An example of dynamical long-range order at M with A0|a1| = 1.0, ω/t = 3.5. (a) Electron energy bands and fillings. (b) Magnetic structure factors. (c) Average scalar magnetic chirality χ̄ vs. τt (red dashed line) and the average angle of nearest-neighbor magnetic moments θ̄ vs. τt (blue solid line). The gray dotted lines are calculated by magnetic moments pointing to vertices of the regular tetrahedron. The inserted is the magnetic configuration in a unit cell. (d) Magnetic structure factors S(M) and 10× S(Γ) vs. τt.

FIG. 2. Phase diagram in A0 and ω space. Different colors label various steady states. 1○FM state. 2○Vortex state. 3○Quasi-long-range order at K. 4○Long-Range Order at K/2 and K′/2. 5○Dynamical Long-Range Order at M. 6○Disordered state.

FIG. 3. Behaviors of electrons and magnetic moments for A0|a1|=2.5, ω/t = 3, 12 – two cases of FM state in term of magnetism. (a) Electron energy bands ϵk and fillings ⟨nk⟩ for ω/t = 3. (b) Magnetic structure factors S(q) peak at Γ in the reciprocal space, the same for both cases. (c) Electron energy bands for ω/t = 12. (d) Magnetic structure factors S(K) and S(Γ) vs. τt.

FIG. 5. An example of vortex state with A0|a1| = 5.5, ω/t = 8.0. (a) Electron energy bands and fillings. (b) Magnetic structure factors. (c) The average angle between nearest-neighbor magnetic moments θ̄ vs. τt. (d) Topological charge Q vs. τt.

FIG. 6. An example of quasi-long-range order at K with A0|a1| = 1.0, ω/t = 11.0. (a) Electron energy bands and fillings. (b) Magnetic structure factors. (c) Magnetic configuration of one sublattice with topological swirling moment texture. (d) The scalar magnetic chirality χijkl at τt = 0 and 2000 is shown in the left and right panel, respectively.

FIG. 7. An example of long-range order at K/2 and K′/2 with A0|a1| = 0.5, ω/t = 4.5. (a) Electron energy bands and fillings. (b) Magnetic structure factors. (c) Magnetic structure factors S(K), S(Γ), S(K/2) vs. τt. A magnetic unit cell plotted by black dashed line is inserted. Magnetic moments representing by red and blue arrows with (3a1,− √ 3a2)/2 shifted are opposite. (d) Scalar Magnetic chirality of upright triangles. The chirality of inverted triangles has the same pattern and is not shown here. One of the magnetic unit cells is indicated by a solid black line, and the points inside mark the positions of the triangular lattice sites. The red and magenta markers denote the upright triangles with opposite signs of χijk.
